description: This is a query to generate application graphs generated from the different logs collected by the app agents.

ID.description: ID is the identifier of the object.
ID.title: ID

appGraphKind.description: Filters by application graph kind. Must not be combined with workloadGroupSetHashes.
appGraphKind.title: appGraphKind

direction.description: Determines the sort order of logs.
direction.title: direction

end.description: The end of the time window in any format supported by https://github.com/araddon/dateparse.
end.title: end

endRelative.description: The relative end of the time window as time.Duration.
endRelative.title: endRelative

includeData.description: If true, includes log data (like proxy, connection or DNS logs) in the generated application graph logs. Note that this can significantly increase the size of the response. At the moment, this is only taken into consideration for the trace ID based application graph generation.
includeData.title: includeData

limit.description: The maximum number of results for logs to return per graph per node per link type.
limit.title: limit

start.description: The start of the time window in any format supported by https://github.com/araddon/dateparse.
start.title: start

startRelative.description: The relative start of the time window as time.Duration.
startRelative.title: startRelative

trace.description: If the generated graph was for a trace ID, then this carries the full trace itself.
trace.title: trace

traceID.description: The Trace ID that the graph is being generated for. If this option is set, only a single application graph will be generated for the trace. Values for appGraphKinds and workloadGroupSetHashes will be ignored.
traceID.title: traceID

workloadGroupSetHashes.description: Filters by hashes of the workload group sets to generate application graphs for. Must not be combined with appGraphKinds.
workloadGroupSetHashes.title: workloadGroupSetHashes

title: AppGraphQuery
